back: A data model is a collection of conceptual tools for describing data, data relationships, data semantics, and consistency constraints. It provides an abstract way to describe the design of a database at the physical, logical, and view levels.

front: What are the two main types of Data Models?
back: 1. Object-Based Data Models (e.g., Entity-Relationship model, Object-Oriented model). 2. Record-Based Data Models (e.g., Relational model, Network model, Hierarchical model).

front: Describe the core components of the Entity-Relationship (E-R) Model.
back: The E-R model is based on a perception of the real world consisting of: 1. Entities (objects, e.g., a customer), 2. Attributes (properties of an entity, e.g., customer_id, name), and 3. Relationships (associations among entities, e.g., a customer 'places' an order).

front: What is the primary focus of the Relational Model?
back: The relational model uses a collection of tables (relations) to represent both data and the relationships among those data. Each table has multiple columns, and each column has a unique name. It focuses on describing data at the logical and view levels, hiding physical-level details.

front: What is the main purpose of a Data Model?
back: The main purpose is to represent data in an understandable way, facilitating communication between database designers, developers, and end-users. It serves as a blueprint for building the database and ensures data is organized logically and consistently.
